details.raw_excerpt:

{
  "summary": "Two groups are solid; the homophones group has a critical flaw with LEER and a problematic word choice.",
  "reviews": [
    {
      "index": 0,
      "approved": true,
      "score": 9,
      "issues": ["MOSS stitch is less universally known than the others but is a legitimate knitting stitch; no overlap with existing verified groups"],
      "repaired_group": null
    },
    {
      "index": 1,
      "approved": false,
      "score": 3,
      "issues": [
        "LEER is not a homophone of a word meaning 'to look' — LEER itself means to look lecherously, so it is its own word, not a homophone of another word meaning 'to look'; the mechanism breaks down entirely for this entry",
        "GAYS as a homophone of GAZE is phonetically valid but the word choice is likely to cause offense and distraction in a puzzle context",
        "PIER/peer works, STAIR/stare works
